instruction: In ship2, finish bootstrapping the runnable React + Vite + TypeScript app in place. Make sure package.json exists, install dependencies, create the src UI with a left document sidebar, a top bar, and a main editor placeholder, and leave working dev/build scripts so the app can be run locally from /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship2.

steps.description: Restore the missing root Vite manifest and configuration files required to make the project runnable in place. Recreate package.json with React, React DOM, Vite, TypeScript, and the Vite React plugin plus working dev/build/preview scripts; restore index.html and the TypeScript/Vite config files expected at the repo root. This directly addresses the prior failure caused by the missing package.json.

Create the actual React application source tree and styling for the requested bootstrap UI: app entrypoint, global styles, and the shell layout containing a left document sidebar, a top bar, and a main editor placeholder. Because the current repository has no src files at all, all source files for the runnable UI need to be created in this step.

# Ship2

Ship2 is a small React + Vite + TypeScript workspace shell with a left document sidebar, a top bar, and a main editor placeholder.

## Local development

From this directory:

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Vite will start a local dev server and print the URL to open in your browser.

## Production build

```bash
npm run build
```

This runs the TypeScript project build and produces the optimized Vite output in `dist/`.

## Preview the production build

```bash
npm run preview
```

This serves the built app locally so you can verify the production bundle.

"scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc -b && vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "lint": "tsc -b --pretty false",
    "type-check": "tsc -b --pretty false"
  },

fileEdits.new_string:
## Local development

From this directory:

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Vite will start a local dev server and print the URL to open in your browser.

## Verification and builds

```bash
npm run lint
npm run type-check
npm run build
```

- `lint` runs the TypeScript project check used by local verification.
- `type-check` runs the same TypeScript validation explicitly.
- `build` runs the TypeScript project build and produces the optimized Vite output in `dist/`.

## Preview the production build

```bash
npm run preview
```

This serves the built app locally so you can verify the production bundle.

tool_input.content:
# Ship2

Ship2 is a small React + Vite + TypeScript workspace shell with a left document sidebar, a top bar, and a main editor placeholder.

## Local development

From this directory:

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Vite will start a local dev server and print the URL to open in your browser.

## Production build

```bash
npm run build
```

This runs the TypeScript project build and produces the optimized Vite output in `dist/`.

## Preview the production build

```bash
npm run preview
```

This serves the built app locally so you can verify the production bundle.

tool_input.new_string:
## Local development

From this directory:

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Vite will start a local dev server and print the URL to open in your browser.

## Verification and builds

```bash
npm run lint
npm run type-check
npm run build
```

- `lint` runs the TypeScript project check used by local verification.
- `type-check` runs the same TypeScript validation explicitly.
- `build` runs the TypeScript project build and produces the optimized Vite output in `dist/`.

## Preview the production build

```bash
npm run preview
```

This serves the built app locally so you can verify the production bundle.
